title: Orientation: Type, Value, constructors
analysis: Task 233 adds Orientation as a first-class type in Reify. ALREADY FULLY IMPLEMENTED ON MAIN: Type::Orientation(usize) exists in ty.rs (line 47) with Display (Orientation{N}), Eq/Hash, and a Type::orientation(n) factory method. Value::Orientation { w, x, y, z } exists in value.rs (line 60) with Display, Ord, Hash, content_hash, and dimension() returning DIMENSIONLESS. All 5 constructors exist in reify-stdlib: orient_identity (no-arg, returns identity quaternion), orient_quaternion(w,x,y,z) (normalizes via normalize_quaternion helper), orient_axis_angle(axis_vec, angle) (extracts 3D vector + trig_input angle, normalizes axis, computes half-angle), orient_euler(convention, a, b, c) (12 convention strings, uses elementary_rotation_quat + quat_mul composition), orient_basis(x,y,z) (validates 3D vectors, checks orthonormality within 1e-6, rejects left-handed bases via determinant, uses Shepperd's method for rotation matrix to quaternion). Export tests exist in exports.rs. 23 tests pass in reify-types, 34 tests pass in reify-stdlib (57 total). No compiler or expr evaluator changes needed since orientation constructors dispatch through the standard eval_builtin path.

steps.description: Write failing tests for Type::Orientation(usize) variant: construction, Display ('Orientation3', 'Orientation2'), Eq/Hash behavior, Type::orientation(n) factory, is_numeric() returns false
Add Orientation(usize) variant to Type enum in ty.rs with Display impl ('Orientation{n}'), Type::orientation(n) factory method, ensure Eq/Hash derive covers it

Write failing tests for Value::Orientation { w, x, y, z }: construction, Display ('orientation(w, x, y, z)'), PartialEq/Ord with NaN/neg-zero edge cases, content_hash determinism, type_tag ordering (tag 15), dimension() returns DIMENSIONLESS
Add Orientation { w: f64, x: f64, y: f64, z: f64 } variant to Value enum in value.rs with Display, PartialEq, Ord (bitwise f64 comparison), content_hash, type_tag=15, dimension()=DIMENSIONLESS
Write export test in exports.rs verifying Type::Orientation(3), Type::orientation(3), and Value::Orientation { w, x, y, z } are publicly accessible from reify_types crate
Write failing test for orient_identity: no-arg returns Value::Orientation { w:1, x:0, y:0, z:0 }, with-args returns Undef
Implement orient_identity in eval_builtin match: check args.is_empty(), return identity quaternion or Undef

Write failing tests for orient_quaternion(w,x,y,z): normalizes unnormalized input, preserves already-normalized, arbitrary values normalize correctly, zero-vector returns Undef, NaN/Inf returns Undef, wrong arg count returns Undef

Implement orient_quaternion in eval_builtin with normalize_quaternion(w,x,y,z) helper: extract 4 f64 args, validate finiteness, compute L2 norm, reject zero norm, divide by norm to produce unit quaternion

Write failing tests for orient_axis_angle(axis, angle): 90deg around Z axis, 180deg around X axis, accepts Angle scalar for angle arg, zero axis returns Undef, non-3D axis returns Undef, wrong arg count returns Undef

Implement orient_axis_angle in eval_builtin: extract 3D vector via tensor_components_f64, extract angle via trig_input, validate axis non-zero, normalize axis, compute half-angle quaternion (cos(a/2), sin(a/2)*axis), normalize result

Write failing tests for orient_euler(convention, a, b, c): xyz single-axis rotation, zyx single-axis, zero angles = identity, invalid convention returns Undef, non-string convention returns Undef, accepts Angle scalars, wrong arg count returns Undef

Implement orient_euler in eval_builtin: match convention string against 12 valid conventions (xyz/xzy/yxz/yzx/zxy/zyx/xyx/xzx/yxy/yzy/zxz/zyz), extract 3 angles via trig_input, implement elementary_rotation_quat + quat_mul helpers, compose rotations in convention order, normalize result

Write failing tests for orient_basis(x_vec, y_vec, z_vec): identity basis, 90deg-rotated basis, non-orthogonal returns Undef, non-3D returns Undef, zero-length returns Undef, wrong arg count returns Undef

Implement orient_basis in eval_builtin: extract 3x3D vectors via tensor_components_f64, validate lengths non-zero, normalize basis vectors, check mutual orthogonality (dot products < 1e-6), check right-handedness (determinant ~ 1.0), convert rotation matrix to quaternion via Shepperd's method, normalize result

Write compound rotation tests for orient_euler: xyz with two nonzero angles, zyx with three nonzero angles, xzx proper Euler compound - verify quaternion components against known reference values within 1e-9 tolerance
Write left-handed basis rejection tests for orient_basis: reflected XY plane basis returns Undef, swapped Y/Z basis returns Undef, near-tolerance right-handed basis passes

design_decisions.decision: Orientation(usize) parameterized by spatial dimension N rather than fixed to 3D
design_decisions.rationale: Allows future 2D orientation support (N=2, stored as angle) while N=3 uses unit quaternion. Consistent with Point { n } and Vector { n } patterns.

design_decisions.decision: Value::Orientation stores quaternion as { w, x, y, z } named fields rather than [f64; 4]
design_decisions.rationale: Named fields are self-documenting (w is scalar part), prevent ordering bugs, and match the codebase pattern (Complex uses { re, im }).

design_decisions.decision: Normalize quaternion on construction in all constructors
design_decisions.rationale: Guarantees unit quaternion invariant. Downstream operations (rotation composition, SLERP) can assume |q|=1 without re-checking.

design_decisions.decision: Return Value::Undef on all validation failures rather than panicking
design_decisions.rationale: Follows project convention (feedback_silent_defaults_pattern): logic errors should be noisy at the evaluation layer, not crash the engine. Undef propagates through dependent expressions.

design_decisions.decision: orient_euler supports 12 conventions (6 Tait-Bryan + 6 proper Euler) via string parameter
design_decisions.rationale: Covers all standard aerospace/robotics conventions. String parameter avoids enum proliferation and matches reify's dynamic evaluation model.

design_decisions.decision: orient_basis validates right-handedness via determinant check (det ~ 1.0)
design_decisions.rationale: Left-handed bases represent reflections, not rotations. Quaternions can only represent proper rotations (SO(3)), so left-handed input is semantically invalid.

design_decisions.decision: Use Shepperd's method for rotation matrix to quaternion conversion in orient_basis
design_decisions.rationale: Numerically stable for all rotation angles, avoids the singularities of the naive trace-based method near 180-degree rotations.
Orientations are pure rotations with no physical dimension. Angles are consumed during construction (via trig_input) but the resulting quaternion is dimensionless.

reuse.how: Extracts f64 components from Vector/Point/Tensor values for axis and basis vector arguments
Extracts angle in radians from either ANGLE Scalar or bare Real, used for orient_axis_angle and orient_euler angle arguments
Shared helper validates finiteness and normalizes quaternion, reused by orient_quaternion, orient_axis_angle, orient_euler, and orient_basis
Used by orient_quaternion to extract numeric components from any numeric Value variant
Macro for comparing Orientation values within floating-point tolerance, reused across all orient_* test functions

reuse.what: Content hash / Ord / Display patterns from Complex variant
reuse.how: Orientation follows same implementation patterns as Complex for PartialEq (bitwise f64), Ord (component-wise), content_hash (hash each f64 as bits), Display
Elementary single-axis rotation quaternion and Hamilton product, composed by orient_euler for arbitrary Euler angle sequences
